[2018-05-20 14:57] 1m2o: you are expected to have 8 green and the ability to set up more
[2018-05-20 14:57] 1m2o: it depends on the situation
[2018-05-20 14:57] 1m2o: like the goal is having zemora click every turn
[2018-05-20 14:59] itsotter: as a rule of thumb you might aim to have 4 conduits up by the time zemora comes online
[2018-05-20 15:01] crash_overlord: Basically the "optimal" way would be to never float green or any other reasource but have 8 conduits on the turn zemo comes in. most games that's basically impossible. 

So what people do is 1 of 2 things:

If theres ways to use green well, you'll end up with ~some green in stockpile and 4ish conduits on the 1st turn of zemo,

If theres no way to use green, people stay on 2 conduits and just buy all of them on the first turn of zemo. or well, as many as is needed to keep firing.
[2018-05-20 15:02] shadourow (Rando): Mmmh... forcefield is a way to use green and you often need them when you go for Zemora, right ?
[2018-05-20 15:03] itsotter: it's not an optimal example, but if you have 4 conduits and 10G on your first zemora turn, you can click zemo and buy 2 conduits, and then you'll have 8G on your next turn (2+6) so you can click it again and buy two more conduits, and then you're at 8 conduits and firing every turn
[2018-05-20 15:03] itsotter: conveniently, this means the extra 8 gold from zemo clicks is going into conduits, so your existing tech setup should let you keep spending the rest of your money as you were
[2018-05-20 15:04] itsotter: it doesn't have to be just like that but this is a better way to think of it than "stockpiling a million green in advance" or "make 8 conduits before zemo finishes"

[2018-05-20 19:20] 1000mh (Henry): I really hope this doesn't come back to bite them in the end. The game is good enough that it deserves a sizeable player base, but the barriers to entry for the multi-player aspect might be off putting to enough potential players that it merits some concern.
[2018-05-20 19:22] unearthly: Its mostly an issue if you didn't like the single player
[2018-05-20 19:22] darkeisbein: the barrier to entry is still much lower than every other competetive multiplayer game tbh
[2018-05-20 19:22] unearthly: Knowledge barrier to entry? Don't agree with that.
[2018-05-20 19:22] unearthly: Price wise? Obviously
[2018-05-20 19:22] shadourow (Rando): Eh ? What kind of competitive games ?
[2018-05-20 19:23] darkeisbein: I mean ccgs you gotta grind real long/pay a lot to be able to play the real game
[2018-05-20 19:23] shadourow (Rando): Maybe RTS are more complicated to begin, but CCG aren't, for example
[2018-05-20 19:23] darkeisbein: and RT stuff you have to get all that muscle memory
[2018-05-20 19:23] darkeisbein: and MoBAs just have an unreal knowledge barrier
[2018-05-20 19:24] unearthly: I don't see how a starter deck in a CCG isn't "the real game"
[2018-05-20 19:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): Considering it's been well established that prismata will be f2p at some point, we are likely talking about skills wise.
[2018-05-20 19:24] unearthly: Anymore than not having the blueprints in casual
[2018-05-20 19:24] unearthly: If you both gave a new player a tier 1 CCG deck or dumped them into Prismata ranked, I'd imagine they'd do better with the former
[2018-05-20 19:25] darkeisbein: I mean besides of being at a disadvantage because you have a bad deck
[2018-05-20 19:25] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): hmm steam rating down to 88%
[2018-05-20 19:25] unearthly: Just saying the game is actual f2p isn't an argument
[2018-05-20 19:25] unearthly: Take someone who only did the campaign and put them into ranked with no  help
[2018-05-20 19:25] apooche: Yeah Arka that was expected going F2P
[2018-05-20 19:26] apooche: It’s like a professor rating when a good prof suddenly has to teach an intro lecture for 500 students
[2018-05-20 19:26] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): yeah now I see why elyot didn´t go F2P right away
[2018-05-20 19:26] wilson0862 (Wilson): "If you both gave a new player a tier 1 CCG deck or dumped them into Prismata ranked, I'd imagine they'd do better with the former"
Not sure what this is supposed to show.
[2018-05-20 19:27] apooche: Ccgs have low barrier to entry if you netdeck?
[2018-05-20 19:27] darkeisbein: but you can't actually net deck if you don't have the cards for it
[2018-05-20 19:27] unearthly: @Wilson We were discussing the barrier to entry, which I think must consider more than simply whether a game is f2p
[2018-05-20 19:28] unearthly: The knowledge barrier for a new player is not something you can trivially discard
[2018-05-20 19:28] wilson0862 (Wilson): But how is that even an apples to apples comparison?
[2018-05-20 19:28] unearthly: My point was that you need to learn a lot before you can really play prismata at a functional level
[2018-05-20 19:28] unearthly: Other games are much simpler and thus easier for new players to pick up and understand
[2018-05-20 19:28] apooche: Apples to apples is not a competitive game :Lul:
[2018-05-20 19:29] awaclus: I always play appless to apples competitively
[2018-05-20 19:29] darkeisbein: yeah I guess your right ... personally I'd rather play a game like prismata where I am held back by being bad at a game at the start then a ccg where I'm held back by not having the cards
[2018-05-20 19:31] apooche: If you compare to competitive games in other spaces where you start with all the tools, like fighting games, I think prismata has a low skill barrier for now
[2018-05-20 19:31] f300xen (f0x): mechanical skill is probably different from game knowledge/strategic skill
[2018-05-20 19:31] wilson0862 (Wilson): The newer you are to a game, the bigger the skill gap between you and the average player.
[2018-05-20 19:31] apooche: Just by virtue of not being in an established genre and not being around that long
[2018-05-20 19:31] wilson0862 (Wilson): That's not a flaw. That's just how it works.

[2018-05-21 04:19] emex: Was a very interesting set: Vivids, Zemora, Mahar, Gauss Fabricator, Militia, Husk, Electrovore and Shadowfang. Bot was P1. I decided to T2 a conduit, but because of the green flavor in the set I thought that buying a blastforge wouldn't do it T3. I T3'd more green economy and T4 I had Mahar + Forcefield against P1 who now had animus on board. On T5 he went for tarsiers, and I think at this point I thought that I would have a four absorb per turn due to me potentially having 3 mahars. I was right, and since no one had even bought a blastforge for T7, the bot heavily invested into forcefields. The game was mine, but quite honestly I thought it was going for a Gauss Fabricator.

messages.author: emex
messages.content: Was a very interesting set: Vivids, Zemora, Mahar, Gauss Fabricator, Militia, Husk, Electrovore and Shadowfang. Bot was P1. I decided to T2 a conduit, but because of the green flavor in the set I thought that buying a blastforge wouldn't do it T3. I T3'd more green economy and T4 I had Mahar + Forcefield against P1 who now had animus on board. On T5 he went for tarsiers, and I think at this point I thought that I would have a four absorb per turn due to me potentially having 3 mahars. I was right, and since no one had even bought a blastforge for T7, the bot heavily invested into forcefields. The game was mine, but quite honestly I thought it was going for a Gauss Fabricator.
